customers.name: Chief Revenue Officer / RevOps Leader
customers.description: Revenue executive responsible for pipeline quality, forecast accuracy, sales productivity, pricing discipline, and cross-functional execution from lead creation through renewal.
careAbout: Higher seller time spent on active deals instead of CRM admin
Forecasts that reflect real pipeline health
Faster quote and approval cycles on complex enterprise deals
Expansion into installed accounts without operational friction
winsThem: One trusted account, contact, and opportunity model
Quote-to-cash workflows that do not require swivel-chair operations
AI and automation that make managers and reps faster without reducing control
theirFear: A pipeline that looks healthy but is operationally weak
Slow approvals and contract cycles causing avoidable slippage
New AI layers added on top of untrusted CRM data

jobsToBeDone.name: Turn CRM from a reporting system into a revenue execution system
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Run the business on one operating model that connects accounts, opportunities, forecasting, pricing, and renewals with enough governance that leaders can act early.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher win rates, better forecast accuracy, shorter quote cycles, and more expansion from the installed base.

steps.description: Keep accounts, contacts, activities, and opportunities complete enough that managers can see real deal health.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Account and Contact Graph
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps buyer, account, and relationship context usable across every revenue workflow.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Lead Capture and Qualification
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns inbound and outbound signals into governed pipeline entry.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Opportunity Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides the deal system of record for sellers and managers.

steps.step: Inspect pipeline and call the number with confidence
steps.description: Use stage discipline, activity signals, and manager inspection to separate real coverage from noise.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Activity and Engagement Capture
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Adds trusted interaction data into the pipeline picture.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Pipeline Inspection and Coaching
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Surfaces coverage gaps and deal risk for managers.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Forecast and Quota Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Combines seller inputs and pipeline signals into a management forecast.

steps.step: Convert winning deals without quote-to-cash drag
steps.description: Move complex deals through configuration, pricing, approvals, contracts, billing, and renewals without side spreadsheets or disconnected approvals.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Quote Assembly and Approval
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Generates controlled quotes and approval paths for complex deals.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Contract and Order Lifecycle
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Carries approved deals into contractual and order execution.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Billing and Subscription Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns booked business into invoices, renewals, and revenue continuity.

customerJourneyStories.name: Move from fragmented pipeline reporting to governed revenue execution
customerJourneyStories.summary: A revenue leader evaluates whether Salesforce can make pipeline inspection, complex quoting, and installed-base expansion more predictable without increasing admin burden.

stages.narrative: Forecast misses and long quote cycles expose weak process control.
The buyer looks for a platform that joins seller workflow, forecasting, and monetization.
They compare data quality, manager workflows, pricing flexibility, and approval control.
They pilot a segment with measurable inspection, quoting, and forecast outcomes.
The platform proves value when forecast calls stabilize and deal cycle time falls.
Expansion continues when revenue, service, and data teams can operate on the same account truth.

top.name: Forecast accuracy within commit range
top.description: Increase quarterly forecast accuracy to 95% of committed revenue.

children.name: Stage hygiene compliance
children.description: Increase stage hygiene compliance to 92%.

children.name: Manager-inspected pipeline share
children.description: Increase manager-inspected pipeline share to 85%.

children.name: Complex quote cycle time
children.description: Reduce median complex quote cycle time to 12 hours.

children.name: Approval turnaround within SLA
children.description: Increase quote approval turnaround within SLA to 90%.

top.name: Multi-cloud revenue expansion rate
top.description: Increase the share of strategic accounts expanding into three or more clouds to 46%.

children.name: Net revenue retention
children.description: Sustain net revenue retention above 108%.

children.name: Installed-base expansion attach
children.description: Increase expansion attach on top 500 accounts to 34%.

children.name: Seller admin time reduction
children.description: Reduce seller admin time by 20%.

children.name: Quote rework rate
children.description: Reduce quote rework rate below 8%.

productStrategy.vision: Give revenue leaders one governed system for pipeline, forecasting, pricing, and renewals so the business can scale without spreadsheet operations.

1_year.focus: Raise forecast trust and reduce quote friction in enterprise segments.
milestones: Ship faster approval patterns for complex quotes

3_year.focus: Make revenue execution API-first and AI-assisted across every deal motion.

businessKPI.northStar: Revenue Cloud attach in strategic accounts

milestones: Expose revenue workflows as reusable APIs
Automate renewal playbooks for installed base

5_year.focus: Operate the full commercial system with trusted human and AI collaboration.

supporting: Revenue Cloud attach in strategic accounts

milestones: Automate routine quote and approval work
Standardize agent governance across revenue teams

customers.name: Enterprise Account Executive / Sales Manager
customers.description: Seller or frontline manager responsible for moving deals forward, maintaining account context, coordinating specialists, and closing complex business without losing time to manual CRM work.
careAbout: Clear next-best actions on active deals
One place to see customer activity and account risk
winsThem: Opportunity workspaces that support real deal progression
Mobile and web access to account context
theirFear: CRM turning into extra admin instead of a selling tool
Waiting on pricing and approvals after customer momentum is already there

jobsToBeDone.name: Advance complex deals without becoming a CRM operator
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Keep the pipeline clean, understand account context, and assemble accurate quotes quickly enough that customers do not stall.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: More selling time, faster proposal turnaround, and clearer manager support on risky deals.

steps.step: See the whole account and opportunity picture
steps.description: Know the buyer network, recent interactions, whitespace, and current deal status without assembling it manually.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Account and Contact Graph
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps relationship context in one place.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Activity and Engagement Capture
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Brings meetings, emails, and other engagement into the record.

steps.step: Work the deal with the right guidance
steps.description: Use stage guidance, coaching, and forecast expectations to focus on the few deals that matter most.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Opportunity Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates the selling workspace for progressing the deal.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Pipeline Inspection and Coaching
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows manager expectations and risk signals.

steps.description: Configure products, apply pricing rules, and route approvals without leaving the flow of work.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Configuration Rules Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Guides valid combinations and bundle logic.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Pricing and Discount Governance
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies pricing policies and discount thresholds.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Quote Assembly and Approval
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Assembles and routes customer-ready quotes.

customerJourneyStories.name: Move from CRM compliance to selling leverage
customerJourneyStories.summary: A seller or frontline manager wants Salesforce to remove admin and accelerate the parts of selling that customers actually feel.

stages.narrative: Manual updates and slow approvals consume selling time.
The team looks for one selling workspace instead of separate CRM and CPQ tools.
They test activity capture, quote speed, and manager inspection workflows.
A pilot measures time saved per rep and quote turnaround improvement.
Adoption sticks when sellers trust the system to help, not monitor only.
The platform remains strategic when account context and commercial workflow stay connected.

top.name: Seller productive selling time
top.description: Increase productive selling time to 68% of seller week.

children.name: Activity auto-capture coverage
children.description: Increase activity auto-capture coverage to 90%.

children.name: Quote first-pass completion
children.description: Increase quote first-pass completion to 88%.

children.name: Pipeline inspection adoption
children.description: Increase weekly pipeline inspection adoption to 85%.

children.name: Next-step completeness on active deals
children.description: Increase next-step completeness to 92%.

top.name: Quota attainment consistency
top.description: Increase the share of sellers landing within plan attainment band to 64%.

children.name: Win rate on qualified pipeline
children.description: Increase win rate on qualified pipeline to 31%.

children.name: Average proposal turnaround
children.description: Reduce average proposal turnaround to 1.5 business days.

children.name: Seller weekly active usage
children.description: Increase weekly active usage to 87%.

children.name: Manager coaching time recovered
children.description: Recover 4 hours per manager per week from admin-heavy inspection work.

productStrategy.vision: Make Salesforce the place where sellers actually work the deal, not where they update the deal after the real work is done.

1_year.focus: Reduce seller admin and speed up complex quote creation.
milestones: Expose more guidance in opportunity workflows

3_year.focus: Give every frontline team AI-assisted selling guidance in the flow of work.
milestones: Embed AI into quoting and follow-up

5_year.focus: Support mixed human and AI revenue teams operating on the same governed customer context.
milestones: Blend service and sales signals into account actioning

customers.name: VP Customer Service / Contact Center Operations Leader
customers.description: Service executive responsible for omnichannel support quality, case resolution speed, agent productivity, self-service containment, and consistent customer experience across digital and assisted channels.
careAbout: Deflecting routine work without hurting customer trust
Giving agents one customer context across channels
winsThem: One service console with routing, history, and knowledge
AI and self-service that solve simple issues cleanly
Entitlement and SLA control built into the workflow
theirFear: Agents spending more time hunting context than solving problems
Chatbots that create extra escalations instead of fewer contacts
Channel growth outpacing routing and staffing discipline

jobsToBeDone.name: Run a connected service operation across humans and AI
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Route work, resolve cases, surface knowledge, and automate routine tasks while keeping customer context and service promises intact.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher service quality, lower handle time, and greater deflection of routine work.

steps.step: Understand the customer and case quickly
steps.description: Pull together prior interactions, account context, and entitlement status before an agent or AI starts working the issue.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Case Intake and Resolution Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates the operational service record.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Interaction History and Customer Timeline
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows the prior customer context across channels.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Entitlement and SLA Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies promises and response rules to the case.

steps.step: Send each contact to the right resolver
steps.description: Route work by skill, channel, urgency, and capacity so the organization responds consistently.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Omnichannel Routing and Capacity
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Matches demand to available agents and queues.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Knowledge and Guided Resolution
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Equips agents and automations with the right answers.

steps.step: Automate what should not reach an agent
steps.description: Offer self-service, agentic flows, and workflow automation for predictable requests and updates.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Self-Service and Digital Engagement
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets customers resolve routine issues directly.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Agentforce and AI Workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles routine actions and assists human agents.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Workflow and Low-Code Automation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Automates after-case and cross-system actions.

customerJourneyStories.name: Move from ticket management to connected service orchestration
customerJourneyStories.summary: A service leader evaluates whether Salesforce can coordinate assisted and automated service work without fragmenting the customer experience.

stages.narrative: Case volume rises faster than service headcount and channels multiply.
The team looks for one service platform instead of separate case, chat, and bot tools.
They compare routing quality, knowledge reuse, AI handoff, and SLA control.
A pilot measures deflection, handling time, and escalation quality.
Value becomes clear when routine demand is absorbed without loss of CSAT.
The platform stays strategic when service signals improve account and product decisions beyond the contact center.

top.description: Increase first-contact resolution to 78% across eligible case types.

children.name: Median time to resolution
children.description: Reduce median time to resolution to 7.5 hours.

children.name: Knowledge-assisted resolution rate
children.description: Increase knowledge-assisted resolution rate to 72%.

children.name: Omnichannel routing within SLA
children.description: Increase omnichannel routing within SLA to 96%.

children.name: Customer self-service success rate
children.description: Increase customer self-service success rate to 42%.

top.name: AI-assisted service leverage
top.description: Increase AI-assisted or AI-resolved service work to 36% of eligible demand.

children.name: Agent handle time reduction
children.description: Reduce average agent handle time by 18%.

children.name: Escalation rate on routine issues
children.description: Reduce escalations on routine issues below 9%.

children.name: Service Cloud expansion rate
children.description: Increase Service Cloud expansion rate in target accounts to 22%.

children.name: Self-service cost-to-serve reduction
children.description: Reduce cost to serve eligible digital contacts by 24%.

productStrategy.vision: Give service teams one connected operating system where human agents, automation, and AI work from the same customer truth.

1_year.focus: Improve routing, knowledge reuse, and safe AI deflection.

customers.name: Salesforce Platform Admin / Enterprise Architect
customers.description: Platform owner responsible for permissions, automation, data quality, integrations, release governance, and safe extension of Salesforce across business units.
careAbout: Safe automation without brittle custom code
Faster release cycles with lower admin risk
winsThem: Identity and consent-aware customer data unification
Clear API and event integration patterns
Governed extensibility for admins and developers
theirFear: Configuration sprawl making each release riskier
AI automation acting on low-trust data or weak permissions
Point integrations creating hidden operational fragility

jobsToBeDone.name: Let the business automate and extend Salesforce without losing control
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Provide a platform layer that business teams can change safely while architecture, identity, and data quality remain governed.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Faster delivery of automations, lower release risk, and better readiness for Data Cloud and AI use cases.

steps.step: Control access and trusted customer identity
steps.description: Keep permissions, profiles, and unified customer records aligned to governance rules.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity, Permissions, and Trust
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Controls who can see, change, and trigger what.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Data Cloud Ingestion and Profile Unification
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Builds the trusted customer profile layer for operations and AI.

steps.description: Publish workflows and agent actions that can be audited, rolled back, and monitored.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Workflow and Low-Code Automation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets admins automate repeatable business tasks.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Agentforce and AI Workflows
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Adds governed AI actions into automation paths.

steps.step: Connect external systems without brittle one-offs
steps.description: Use APIs, events, and reusable extension patterns rather than ad hoc integrations for every team.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: API, Event, and Integration Hub
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides reusable connection patterns across enterprise systems.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Operational Analytics and Insights
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Measures adoption, process quality, and automation health.

customerJourneyStories.name: Move from configuration sprawl to governed platform operations
customerJourneyStories.summary: An admin or architect wants Salesforce to remain flexible enough for fast business change without becoming an ungovernable customization estate.

stages.narrative: Every new automation or integration increases perceived release risk.
The team looks for patterns that keep low-code speed but improve governance.
They compare permission models, integration patterns, release tooling, and observability.
A pilot measures how quickly teams can publish new automation safely.
The platform earns trust when business teams can change workflows without architecture debt spiking.
The platform becomes a durable standard when new AI and data use cases can be adopted without re-platforming.
